writeup: June 8, 2026 landed squarely in the quiet gap between the Bills' final OTA session (June 4) and the opening of mandatory minicamp (June 9), leaving the Buffalo news cycle in a reflective holding pattern. The dominant storyline of the moment was analytical: Buffalo Rumblings ran a detailed piece predicting Josh Allen's target-share distribution and passing statistics under new head coach Joe Brady and offensive coordinator Pete Carmichael, arguing that the ball-control system of the Sean McDermott era would give way to a more expansive attack. Alongside that, the franchise-wide transition narrative — first year under Brady, new defensive coordinator Jim Leonhard, and the imminent debut of the brand-new Highmark Stadium — dominated offseason discourse. Bills Mafia was eager but patient, knowing the real answers would come just 24 hours later when mandatory minicamp opened its doors.

articles.title: Predicting Buffalo Bills target shares, Josh Allen's statistics in 2026
